rationale: Falsifiability/AC-quality lens. All 7 ACs are runnable: AC1-6 use `go test -run 'Name$' | grep -q -- '--- PASS: Name'` (a misnamed/failing/non-compiling test can't emit the PASS line, so no false-pass); AC7 is a build+regression exit-0 gate (appropriate, no single test to grep). The brief's four focus areas are airtight: AC3 covers ALL R5 refusals including the new threshold-bounds (0/negative AND >reviewer-count); AC1 covers the new halt/budget defaults (3/2/2/halt, budget 0/0 unset); AC4 covers R7 absent/n-1/in-range/out-of-range(0/neg/>N)/garbage, incl. 'recorded 0 never yields 0'. R2/R6/R8/R10 map cleanly. Two genuine uncovered falsification clauses remain: (1) R3 literally states 'Falsified if a populated models: map does not round-trip through Load' but no AC ever loads a populated models map (AC1/AC6 test only the empty default); (2) R1's content falsifications (four layers, ratchet direction, precedent chain, portability/artifact-contract) have no runnable check—only `grep Status: Accepted` in Validation Proofs. Both are cheap, additive fixes, not architectural; hence REQUEST_CHANGES, not REJECT, no hard_block.
concrete_changes_required: R3 coverage: add a runnable AC (or extend AC1/AC2) that loads a config with a populated `models:` map (e.g. {implement: <id>, review: <id>}) and asserts it round-trips through Load unchanged — the spec's own 'Falsified if a populated models: map does not round-trip' clause currently has zero test exercising a non-empty map. While there, consider asserting a populated `panel.reviewers` custom-count list and a populated valid `loop` block also round-trip (R2's non-dropped list is only indirectly proven via AC2's sum; R4's parse is only proven via AC3's refusals).

R1 coverage: promote the `grep -q 'Status: Accepted' .mindspec/adr/ADR-0040-*.md` check into the Acceptance-Criteria checklist (not just Validation Proofs), and add greppable content anchors for the substantive R1 falsifications — e.g. grep the ADR for each of the four layer labels (L1/L2/L3/L4), a 'ratchet'/'never casually up' direction phrase, and a 'portability'/'artifact + CLI contract' phrase — so R1's content clauses (four layers, ratchet direction, precedent chain, portability principle) have a runnable falsifier rather than relying solely on Bead 1's own panel read.
findings: R↔AC matrix complete. Fully covered: R2 (AC1 defaults + AC2 custom-sum proves list not dropped), R4 (AC1 defaults + AC3 proves loop parsed), R5 (AC3 — all 7 refusal inputs incl. threshold 0/neg/>count), R6 (AC2 raw-expr-unresolved + sum), R7 (AC4 — absent/n-1/in-range/out-of-range/garbage, recorded-0-never-0), R8 (AC5 decision-invariance + note-never-flips; go list -deps import ban), R10 (AC1 default + AC3 unknown-runner-errors + AC6 surfaced).

GAP (moderate) R3: 'Falsified if a populated models: map does not round-trip through Load' — no AC loads a non-empty models map; AC1 and AC6 both test the empty default only. The default-empty half is covered; the populated-round-trip half is untested.

GAP (moderate) R1: content falsification clauses (four layers present, ratchet direction, precedent chain, portability/artifact-contract principle) have no runnable check. Only `grep Status: Accepted` (Validation Proofs, not the AC checklist) exists, which covers 'file absent' + 'not Accepted' but none of the four content requirements.

SOFT R7: the 'no second interpreter is introduced anywhere' clause is a code-review invariant, not AC-checkable; AC4 covers only the behavioral half. Acceptable but note it relies on panel read, not a runnable proof (unlike R8's import-ban which IS proven by `go list -deps`).

SOFT R9: 'mutates any file' and 'exits non-zero on a valid config' are covered only in Validation Proofs (`git status` no-change + `exit 0`), not the AC checklist; AC6 tests only the pure `renderConfig`. The 'omits any new key' half (panel/models/loop/runner) IS covered by AC6. Acceptable.

POSITIVE: the grep-PASS-line AC pattern is robust — anchored `-run 'Name$'` plus `grep -q -- '--- PASS: Name'` means a renamed/deleted test yields 'no tests to run' (exit 0) but produces no PASS line, so the AC still fails. No false-pass surface.
POSITIVE: AC7's `go build ./... && go test <touched pkgs>` is the correct exit-0 regression gate; the spec correctly relegates the full `go test ./...` sweep to plan time (stated in AC7).
